title: Pakistan Says It Destroyed India's S-400. The Satellites Say Otherwise.
narrative: Pakistan's Information Ministry and Air Force have repeatedly claimed, via official statements and edited propaganda videos, that Operation Sindoor successfully destroyed India's S-400 Triumf air defense systems at multiple air bases including Bhuj and Adampur. The Pakistani government cited satellite images presented in a formal press briefing as proof.
findings: High-resolution commercial satellite imagery shows India's S-400 components at Bhuj Air Base — including the 91N6E Big Bird acquisition radar, 92N6E Grave Stone engagement radar, and Cheese Board early warning system — all in perfect condition with no damage
The Pakistani satellite images have been independently assessed as either fabricated or misattributed
Pakistan's PIB fact-check unit also flagged the viral images as fake and from 2010
IDF/IAFE WRU's Campbell Bay imagery further confirms operational status of Indian systems
Similar claims about damage to other Indian airbases have been repeatedly debunked by independent satellite verification
No correction from the US, which has a strategic interest in supporting Pakistan in the current conflict
telemetry: S-400 systems at Bhuj confirmed operational via commercial satellite — May 2026
Pakistani government satellite images assessed as fabricated or from 2010
Zero US correction on Pakistan's false claims despite having ISR assets in the region
verdict: Another week, another batch of destruction claims from Pakistan's military that falls apart under the scrutiny of commercially available satellite data. The S-400 claims are a case study in information warfare: official government video presentations with fabricated evidence, distributed through state media, amplified internationally — and no correction mechanism.
